result:
Confirmed and verified against source. Answer below.

## `struct list_head` value fields directly in `task_struct` body (`include/linux/sched.h:743`–`1554`)

| # | Field | Line | Guard |
|---|-------|------|-------|
| 1 | `rcu_node_entry` | 847 | `#ifdef CONFIG_PREEMPT_RCU` |
| 2 | `rcu_tasks_holdout_list` | 856 | `#ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_RCU` |
| 3 | `trc_holdout_list` | 863 | `#ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| 4 | `trc_blkd_node` | 864 | `#ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| 5 | `tasks` | 870 | none (unconditional) |
| 6 | `children` | 989 | none (unconditional) |
| 7 | `sibling` | 990 | none (unconditional) |
| 8 | `ptraced` | 999 | none (unconditional) |
| 9 | `ptrace_entry` | 1000 | none (unconditional) |
| 10 | `thread_group` | 1005 | none (unconditional) |
| 11 | `thread_node` | 1006 | none (unconditional) |
| 12 | `cg_list` | 1227 | `#ifdef CONFIG_CGROUPS` |
| 13 | `pi_state_list` | 1238 | `#ifdef CONFIG_FUTEX` |
| 14 | `perf_event_list` | 1246 | `#ifdef CONFIG_PERF_EVENTS` |

**Total: 14**

**#ifdef handling:** 8 of the 14 are gated (counted, not excluded) — `CONFIG_PREEMPT_RCU` (1), `CONFIG_TASKS_RCU` (1), `CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` (2), `CONFIG_CGROUPS` (1), `CONFIG_FUTEX` (1), `CONFIG_PERF_EVENTS` (1). The other 6 are unconditional. All are counted regardless of guard since they're all declared members of the type — the config gate only affects whether they exist in a *given build*, not whether they're a field of the struct definition as written.

**Unnamed structs/unions:** none. I scanned the whole body (`awk` for anonymous `struct {`/`union {` blocks between lines 743–1554) and found no anonymous aggregate wrapping any field — every `list_head` sits directly at the top level of `task_struct`, so no field needed special "inside unnamed struct/union" treatment.

**Excluded near-misses (verified by type, not name):** `preempt_notifiers` (826, `hlist_head`), `kretprobe_instances` (1509) and `rethooks` (1512) (`llist_head`) — different list types, not `list_head`.

Cross-check: a scoped `grep -n "struct list_head"` over exactly lines 743–1554 returned the same 14 hits, none with a `*` (i.e., all value fields, no pointers).

## Real iteration (kernel/)

`kernel/exit.c:471`
```c
list_for_each_entry(c, &p->children, sibling) {
        if (c->mm == mm)
                goto assign_new_owner;
}
```
Iterates `p->children` (field #6) using the embedded `sibling` link (field #7) as the `list_head` member — the standard parent→children walk pattern also used at `kernel/exit.c:479,702,1499` and `kernel/fork.c:3223`.
